objective: Strategie de Mean-reversion sur ALTUSDC 1M. Indicateurs : Bollinger Bands(20,2), ATR. Entree short quand prix rejete depuis bande superieure vers bande mediane. Sortie a la mediane. Stop-loss = 1.5x ATR, take-profit = 3.0x ATR.

iterations.hypothesis: Fallback contractuel: proposition générée automatiquement pour maintenir la progression quand la sortie LLM n'est pas exploitable.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] The strategy is based on mean-reversion and uses Bollinger Bands, ATR indicators for entry/exit signals. The fallback contract will help in maintaining the progression when LLM is not exploitable. Potential weaknesses could be related to market conditions or unforeseen factors affecting the performance of the strategy.
[Backup plan] ['1) If results are poor (negative return), try adjusting stop-loss and take profit parameters. 2) For mediocre returns, adjust RSI, EMA indicators for better signal confirmation. 3) If these changes do not improve performance, consider incorporating other market conditions or volume data into the strategy.']

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] The strategy uses Bollinger Bands and ATR to identify mean reversion opportunities in 0GUSDC 1h. The entry is triggered when price rejects the upper band for the median, while exit occurs at the median with stop-loss equal to 1.5x ATR and take-profit equal to 3.0x ATR. If results are poor (negative return), a parameter adjustment could be increasing the lookback period or adding an additional filter. For mediocre returns (low Sharpe), adjusting stop loss, take profit points may help improve performance. In case of failure, trading another asset class might be considered.
[Backup plan] 1. Increase lookback period to 60 bars and change the trailing stop-loss to trail the ATR.2. Introduce additional filters like RSI or VWAP for improved signal accuracy.3. Adjust take profit point to 4x ATR, as it may help lock in more profits.4. If performance is still poor after these adjustments, consider adding a filter based on volume data.
